final_idea_spec:
hypothesis: "When average next-token entropy is held approximately constant, truncation-based decoding (top-k or top-p) will still produce more short-range self-overlap than smooth full-support temperature scaling. This would suggest that repetition in LLM-like text is driven by tail truncation itself, not only by lower token uncertainty."
intervention_family: "model_behavior"
target_component: "Decoding-rule effects on repeated n-gram statistics in locally generated autoregressive text"
implementation_scope:
  lines_estimate: 190
  dependencies:
    - "numpy"
    - "scipy"
    - "matplotlib"
  compute: "cpu"
  time_estimate_min: 5
proxy_evaluation:
  metric: "self_overlap_auc"
  baseline: "Full-support temperature sampling tuned to match the average next-token entropy of the truncation conditions"
  success_threshold: "Either top-k or top-p yields at least 15% higher self_overlap_auc than the entropy-matched baseline, with a bootstrap 95% confidence interval above 0."
full_evaluation:
  dataset: "A fully local synthetic corpus of 240 documents (60 per condition, 400 tokens each) generated from a Zipfian topic-switching autoregressive token model with sentence-boundary tokens; conditions are temperature-1 reference, entropy-matched full-support temperature sampling, top-k sampling, and top-p sampling."
  n_samples: 240
  n_conditions: 4
expected_failure_modes:
  - "The synthetic generator may be too simple, causing all decoding rules to look similar."
  - "Entropy matching across decoding strategies may be imperfect, confounding the comparison."
  - "Document length may be too short for stable higher-order n-gram repetition estimates."
  - "If the base distribution is already very peaked, top-k and top-p may not alter the sequence statistics much."
rollback_plan: "If entropy matching is unstable, switch to matched perplexity instead of matched entropy and keep the same overlap analysis. If repeated n-grams are too sparse, reduce vocabulary size or increase document length slightly while staying under the runtime budget; if effects remain weak, use longest repeated n-gram length and repetition burst index as secondary statistics."

score_progression.key_finding: Explicit numbering modestly improved performance over flat prose in the surrogate benchmark (exact match 0.307 -> 0.337; precedence error 0.026 -> 0.018), but the effect was far smaller than the preregistered success threshold, and adding a recap did not provide additional benefit.
